headline: Anti-Stalking Act protects 1,200 victims in first year
context: The Anti-Stalking Act, passed in 2024, resulted in 1,200 protection orders issued in its first year of enforcement.

cards.big: 1,200 anti-stalking protection orders issued in first year since 2024 enactment
cards.sub: Law fills gap that left victims without specific legal remedy

cards.big: Anti-Stalking Act 2024 carries penalties up to 3 years jail and RM10,000 fine
cards.sub: Covers physical and cyber stalking with emergency protection orders available

cards.big: Prior to the Act, stalking victims relied on Penal Code Section 509 on insult
cards.sub: Dedicated legislation recognises stalking as distinct pattern of behaviour

cards.big: 87% of protection order applicants were women aged 20-40
cards.sub: Digital stalking comprised 43% of all complaints filed

cards.big: 1,200 orders in year one shows massive unmet need that existed before the law
Enforcement challenges remain with only 78 prosecutions from 1,200 orders
